behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the lint rule logic itself (string/AST matching for leaked phase instructions). It can be caught without real harness wiring, model calls, or OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the lint function/template checker a template containing copied phase instructions and assert it fails with a leakage error; feed a clean template and assert pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: The bug appears at module handoff: template + fixtures + interpolation + assembler. A unit test of the template alone can miss leakage introduced during assembly.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-building path with production-like fixtures, capture the final assembled prompt string, and assert no forbidden phase-instruction spans from SKILL.md appear.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is a local wiring and data-source provenance check across loader, file-backed skill context, and invocation builder. No real LLM is required to prove context source is file-driven.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real invocation path with actual plugin/skill files; assert the loaded context includes expected SKILL.md-derived segments and that no equivalent hard-coded phase rules are injected from test code.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the real model’s behavior changing when skill context quality regresses. Mocked outputs can hide this causal dependency and tautology risk.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B test invocations against the real model using identical user-style prompts: baseline skill content vs degraded/removed section. Assert baseline meets structured criteria while degraded variant fails those same criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across components (model response handler, artifact writer, parser). The key failure is interaction sequencing, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real harness path with a controlled model response; verify raw text artifact is written first and remains available even when downstream JSON parsing intentionally fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is assertion logic plus extraction/validation modules together. A single-function keyword check test is insufficient; the test must exercise real assertion wiring against representative outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the assertion pipeline two outputs: (1) keyword-only echo lacking required structure, (2) properly structured output. Assert (1) fails schema/placement checks and (2) passes.
